<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11263357]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ref improve|date=October 10, 2015}} {{otheruses |festival at Susukigawa Shrine in Matsumoto, Nagano Prefecture |festival at {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|1=Hotaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597109}} in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|1=Azumino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534667}} |Hotaka Shrine#御船祭 }} {{Pathnav|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|frame=1}} [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An “ofune” passing energetically beneath the torii]]
'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.
==Overview== The festival traditionally announces the start of {{ill|Rice planting|ja|田植え|1=rice transplantation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12595244}} in the Yamabe area and prays for an abundant {{ill|Harvest|ja|豊作|1=Bumper crop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4997332}} in autumn. Because Susukigawa Shrine’s deities are not sea-gods, the custom began only in the Edo period, modelled on maritime rites elsewhere.
Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.
==Object of Worship== The Yamabe district lies along the {{ill|Usu River|ja|薄川|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}}, which has long been revered. The Ofune Matsuri is believed to carry the river’s blessings to every neighbourhood.
An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.
==About the “ofune”==
===Structure=== Each float is roughly 5 m high, 2.82 m wide, and 4.44 m deep (dimensions vary by association). For the festival a carrying-pole is lashed to either side and curtains are draped all around so that the float sways like a great ship, making it look even larger. There is only one axle with a pair of wheels. The second storey is open on all sides, while the first is richly carved; the axle bed is lavishly lacquered and fitted with ornate metalwork. Some floats have roofs (seri) that can be raised and lowered. This style of float is extremely rare nationwide.
===Specifications=== The floats are described below in the order in which they proceed.
====Usu-machi==== [[File:薄町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Usu-machi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyamoto'' or ''Kataha-sha'' * '''Carver''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige|ja|立川和四郎富重}}, {{ill|Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane|ja|立川専四郎富種}} and his daughter Matsushiro (Shōran) * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Li Tieguai|ja|李鉄拐|lt=Tekkai Sennin|lt_ja=鐡拐仙人|1=Iron-Crutch Li|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1473554}}, 唐獅子 (Chinese lion), {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, “Millet Ears with Quail,” {{ill|Gama Sennin|ja|蝦蟇仙人|1=Gama Sennin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625785}}, {{ill|Dongfang Shuo|ja|東方朔|1=Dongfang Shuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190966}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Like Yunohara, especially rich in carvings. A plan to display a figure of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} on the second storey was never completed. {{-}}
====Yunohara==== [[File:湯の原のお船（せり上げ前）.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yunohara ofune (roof lowered)]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Shiraito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige; Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane (Takusai) * '''Built''' : Started Ansei 2 (1855), completed Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Total cost''' : 736 ryō 3 bu + 108 mon * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|ja|二十四孝|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1851166}} (Tang Lady nursing her mother, Yang Xiang and a tiger, Tan Zi and the hunter, Emperor Shun and an elephant), “Millet Ears with Quail,” lions, dragons, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From the top—blue-green, red, yellow-green * '''Features''' : Roof is a raisable “seri.” Extremely elaborate carving; black lacquered axle bed; gold-leaf coffered ceiling on the second storey. The green metalwork, showing dragons and clouds, is by Kyoto artisan Taia-mi Sakuzaemon. The Yunohara youths who haul this float are famous for their spirit and form the festival’s core. {{-}}
====Arai==== [[File:新井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Arai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Wakakusa-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Chinese lion, {{ill|Seven Sages|ja|七賢人|1=Seven Sages of Greece|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207782}}, {{ill|Ma Shihuang|ja|馬師皇}}, dragon, carp in a torrent, tortoise in a torrent, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Relief-style carving distinct from the Tachikawa school; roof is raisable. {{-}}
====Shimokanai==== [[File:下金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shimokanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kaneko-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Harada Sōkei, disciple of Tomitane * '''Built''' : Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q32613}}, {{ill|Tokiwa Gozen|ja|常盤御前|1=Tokiwa Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q867354}}, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Ushiwakamaru|lt_ja=牛若丸}} and {{ill|Musashibō Benkei|ja|武蔵坊弁慶|lt=Benkei|lt_ja=弁慶|1=Musashibō Benkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q316898}} on Gojō Bridge, “Millet Ears with Quail,” bamboo and tiger, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Gold-leaf “cloud ceiling” beneath the eaves of the second storey; “Kemari-shishi” lions (ceiling); “Elegant Gentlemen at Leisure” (lower tier) are original. {{-}}
====Aramachi==== [[File:荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Akabito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Tōkei|ja|立川東渓}} (Shimizu Tōkei) and his son Shimizu Yūsui (1926) * '''Built''' : Probably early Meiji; one account claims Meiwa 4 (1767), but no reliable source confirms this. * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}}, Minamoto no Yoritomo, {{ill|Nio|ja|力神}}, {{ill|Fenghuang|ja|鳳凰|1=fenghuang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q527525}}, {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}}, {{ill|Fūjin|ja|風神}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Most carvings are by Tōkei. The purple dragon curtain on the second storey was sewn in Bunsei 12 (1829). In Meiji 2 (1869) a red foreign broadcloth curtain was imported from Britain and added. {{-}}
====Nishi-Aramachi==== [[File:西荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nishi-Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kayano-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Shimizu Yūsui * '''Built''' : Taishō 11 (1922) * '''Carvings''' : ''Kuni-umashi Monogatari'', ''Kamado no Kemuri'', dragons, birds-and-flowers, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. {{-}}
====Kamikanai==== [[File:上金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kamikanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Enjō-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Tachikawa Tōkei (mid-Meiji) * '''Built''' : Before Tenpō 6 (1835) * '''Carvings''' : Ascending dragon, descending dragon, carp in rapids, tortoise in waves, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : The only ofune that displays a figure ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}) on the second storey. The ascending/descending dragons on the roof pillars are especially striking. Probably the oldest float on record. {{-}}
====Fujii==== [[File:藤井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fujii ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Asahi-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Tomiyasu * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Hotei and karako children, Bishamonten and Benzaiten, {{ill|Lin Hejing|ja|林和靖|1=Lin Bu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q285429}}, immortals, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From top—red, black, red, white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Underwent major repairs in 2007–08. {{-}}
====Tokawaji==== [[File:兎川寺のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tokawaji ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyako-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unspecified Tachikawa-school artists * '''Supplementary carving''' : Shimizu Shimata-rō, second son of Tōkei (1928) * '''Built''' : Around the Tenpō era (1830s?) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木公}}, {{ill|Murakami Yoshimitsu|ja|村上義光|1=Murakami Yoshiteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522887}}, treasuries motif, {{ill|Lu Ao|ja|盧敖}}, mice and daikon radish, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Underwent major repairs in 2023–24. {{-}}
==Issues== * More than a century has passed since most floats were built, and carvings are cracked or broken; wheels tilt and overall damage is severe. Restoration is extremely costly and beyond the means of the individual neighbourhood associations. * In the 2000s outsiders began boarding floats and committing acts of violence, disrupting the festival. In 2014 the organising committee adopted a resolution excluding {{ill|Japanese organized crime group|ja|暴力団|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465|lt=organized crime groups|1=Japanese organized crime group}} and strictly forbade anyone but designated participants from boarding.<ref>“昨年に続き暴力排除決議 実行委と祭典委.” 『長野日報』25 June 2015.</ref>
==Notes== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==External links== * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1+index.id+78.htm Susukigawa Shrine Grand Festival “Ofune Matsuri”] – Official Matsumoto tourism site * [http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/prefecture/058.html Satoyamabe Ofune Matsuri Floats] – “Treasures of Matsumoto” (Matsumoto City Board of Education) * [http://www3.ncp.or.jp/cgi-shl/pbcgi60J.exe/BUNKA/u_tools/f_dsp_bz/?2938 Satoyamabe Ofune Festival Floats]{{リンク切れ|date=March 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }} – Nagano Cultural Properties (Hachijūni Cultural Foundation) * {{YouTube|ayNkT8kd5oM|Ofune Nine Boats Entering the Shrine}}
{{event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofune Matsuri}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11263357]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ref improve|date=October 10, 2015}} {{otheruses |festival at Susukigawa Shrine in Matsumoto, Nagano Prefecture |festival at {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|1=Hotaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597109}} in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|1=Azumino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534667}} |Hotaka Shrine#御船祭 }} {{Pathnav|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|frame=1}} [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An “ofune” passing energetically beneath the torii]]
'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.
==Overview== The festival traditionally announces the start of {{ill|Rice planting|ja|田植え|1=rice transplantation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12595244}} in the Yamabe area and prays for an abundant {{ill|Harvest|ja|豊作|1=Bumper crop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4997332}} in autumn. Because Susukigawa Shrine’s deities are not sea-gods, the custom began only in the Edo period, modelled on maritime rites elsewhere.
Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.
==Object of Worship== The Yamabe district lies along the {{ill|Usu River|ja|薄川|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}}, which has long been revered. The Ofune Matsuri is believed to carry the river’s blessings to every neighbourhood.
An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.
==About the “ofune”==
===Structure=== Each float is roughly 5 m high, 2.82 m wide, and 4.44 m deep (dimensions vary by association). For the festival a carrying-pole is lashed to either side and curtains are draped all around so that the float sways like a great ship, making it look even larger. There is only one axle with a pair of wheels. The second storey is open on all sides, while the first is richly carved; the axle bed is lavishly lacquered and fitted with ornate metalwork. Some floats have roofs (seri) that can be raised and lowered. This style of float is extremely rare nationwide.
===Specifications=== The floats are described below in the order in which they proceed.
====Usu-machi==== [[File:薄町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Usu-machi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyamoto'' or ''Kataha-sha'' * '''Carver''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige|ja|立川和四郎富重}}, {{ill|Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane|ja|立川専四郎富種}} and his daughter Matsushiro (Shōran) * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Li Tieguai|ja|李鉄拐|lt=Tekkai Sennin|lt_ja=鐡拐仙人|1=Iron-Crutch Li|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1473554}}, 唐獅子 (Chinese lion), {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, “Millet Ears with Quail,” {{ill|Gama Sennin|ja|蝦蟇仙人|1=Gama Sennin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625785}}, {{ill|Dongfang Shuo|ja|東方朔|1=Dongfang Shuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190966}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Like Yunohara, especially rich in carvings. A plan to display a figure of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} on the second storey was never completed. {{-}}
====Yunohara==== [[File:湯の原のお船（せり上げ前）.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yunohara ofune (roof lowered)]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Shiraito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige; Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane (Takusai) * '''Built''' : Started Ansei 2 (1855), completed Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Total cost''' : 736 ryō 3 bu + 108 mon * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|ja|二十四孝|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1851166}} (Tang Lady nursing her mother, Yang Xiang and a tiger, Tan Zi and the hunter, Emperor Shun and an elephant), “Millet Ears with Quail,” lions, dragons, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From the top—blue-green, red, yellow-green * '''Features''' : Roof is a raisable “seri.” Extremely elaborate carving; black lacquered axle bed; gold-leaf coffered ceiling on the second storey. The green metalwork, showing dragons and clouds, is by Kyoto artisan Taia-mi Sakuzaemon. The Yunohara youths who haul this float are famous for their spirit and form the festival’s core. {{-}}
====Arai==== [[File:新井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Arai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Wakakusa-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Chinese lion, {{ill|Seven Sages|ja|七賢人|1=Seven Sages of Greece|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207782}}, {{ill|Ma Shihuang|ja|馬師皇}}, dragon, carp in a torrent, tortoise in a torrent, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Relief-style carving distinct from the Tachikawa school; roof is raisable. {{-}}
====Shimokanai==== [[File:下金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shimokanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kaneko-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Harada Sōkei, disciple of Tomitane * '''Built''' : Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q32613}}, {{ill|Tokiwa Gozen|ja|常盤御前|1=Tokiwa Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q867354}}, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Ushiwakamaru|lt_ja=牛若丸}} and {{ill|Musashibō Benkei|ja|武蔵坊弁慶|lt=Benkei|lt_ja=弁慶|1=Musashibō Benkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q316898}} on Gojō Bridge, “Millet Ears with Quail,” bamboo and tiger, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Gold-leaf “cloud ceiling” beneath the eaves of the second storey; “Kemari-shishi” lions (ceiling); “Elegant Gentlemen at Leisure” (lower tier) are original. {{-}}
====Aramachi==== [[File:荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Akabito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Tōkei|ja|立川東渓}} (Shimizu Tōkei) and his son Shimizu Yūsui (1926) * '''Built''' : Probably early Meiji; one account claims Meiwa 4 (1767), but no reliable source confirms this. * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}}, Minamoto no Yoritomo, {{ill|Nio|ja|力神}}, {{ill|Fenghuang|ja|鳳凰|1=fenghuang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q527525}}, {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}}, {{ill|Fūjin|ja|風神}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Most carvings are by Tōkei. The purple dragon curtain on the second storey was sewn in Bunsei 12 (1829). In Meiji 2 (1869) a red foreign broadcloth curtain was imported from Britain and added. {{-}}
====Nishi-Aramachi==== [[File:西荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nishi-Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kayano-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Shimizu Yūsui * '''Built''' : Taishō 11 (1922) * '''Carvings''' : ''Kuni-umashi Monogatari'', ''Kamado no Kemuri'', dragons, birds-and-flowers, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. {{-}}
====Kamikanai==== [[File:上金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kamikanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Enjō-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Tachikawa Tōkei (mid-Meiji) * '''Built''' : Before Tenpō 6 (1835) * '''Carvings''' : Ascending dragon, descending dragon, carp in rapids, tortoise in waves, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : The only ofune that displays a figure ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}) on the second storey. The ascending/descending dragons on the roof pillars are especially striking. Probably the oldest float on record. {{-}}
====Fujii==== [[File:藤井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fujii ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Asahi-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Tomiyasu * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Hotei and karako children, Bishamonten and Benzaiten, {{ill|Lin Hejing|ja|林和靖|1=Lin Bu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q285429}}, immortals, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From top—red, black, red, white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Underwent major repairs in 2007–08. {{-}}
====Tokawaji==== [[File:兎川寺のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tokawaji ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyako-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unspecified Tachikawa-school artists * '''Supplementary carving''' : Shimizu Shimata-rō, second son of Tōkei (1928) * '''Built''' : Around the Tenpō era (1830s?) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木公}}, {{ill|Murakami Yoshimitsu|ja|村上義光|1=Murakami Yoshiteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522887}}, treasuries motif, {{ill|Lu Ao|ja|盧敖}}, mice and daikon radish, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Underwent major repairs in 2023–24. {{-}}
==Issues== * More than a century has passed since most floats were built, and carvings are cracked or broken; wheels tilt and overall damage is severe. Restoration is extremely costly and beyond the means of the individual neighbourhood associations. * In the 2000s outsiders began boarding floats and committing acts of violence, disrupting the festival. In 2014 the organising committee adopted a resolution excluding {{ill|Japanese organized crime group|ja|暴力団|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465|lt=organized crime groups|1=Japanese organized crime group}} and strictly forbade anyone but designated participants from boarding.<ref>“昨年に続き暴力排除決議 実行委と祭典委.” 『長野日報』25 June 2015.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11263357]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ref improve|date=October 10, 2015}} {{otheruses |festival at Susukigawa Shrine in Matsumoto, Nagano Prefecture |festival at {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|1=Hotaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597109}} in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|1=Azumino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534667}} |Hotaka Shrine#御船祭 }} {{Pathnav|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|frame=1}} [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An “ofune” passing energetically beneath the torii]]
'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.
==Overview== The festival traditionally announces the start of {{ill|Rice planting|ja|田植え|1=rice transplantation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12595244}} in the Yamabe area and prays for an abundant {{ill|Harvest|ja|豊作|1=Bumper crop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4997332}} in autumn. Because Susukigawa Shrine’s deities are not sea-gods, the custom began only in the Edo period, modelled on maritime rites elsewhere.
Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.
==Object of Worship== The Yamabe district lies along the {{ill|Usu River|ja|薄川|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}}, which has long been revered. The Ofune Matsuri is believed to carry the river’s blessings to every neighbourhood.
An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.
==About the “ofune”==
===Structure=== Each float is roughly 5 m high, 2.82 m wide, and 4.44 m deep (dimensions vary by association). For the festival a carrying-pole is lashed to either side and curtains are draped all around so that the float sways like a great ship, making it look even larger. There is only one axle with a pair of wheels. The second storey is open on all sides, while the first is richly carved; the axle bed is lavishly lacquered and fitted with ornate metalwork. Some floats have roofs (seri) that can be raised and lowered. This style of float is extremely rare nationwide.
===Specifications=== The floats are described below in the order in which they proceed.
====Usu-machi==== [[File:薄町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Usu-machi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyamoto'' or ''Kataha-sha'' * '''Carver''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige|ja|立川和四郎富重}}, {{ill|Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane|ja|立川専四郎富種}} and his daughter Matsushiro (Shōran) * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Li Tieguai|ja|李鉄拐|lt=Tekkai Sennin|lt_ja=鐡拐仙人|1=Iron-Crutch Li|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1473554}}, 唐獅子 (Chinese lion), {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, “Millet Ears with Quail,” {{ill|Gama Sennin|ja|蝦蟇仙人|1=Gama Sennin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625785}}, {{ill|Dongfang Shuo|ja|東方朔|1=Dongfang Shuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190966}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Like Yunohara, especially rich in carvings. A plan to display a figure of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} on the second storey was never completed. {{-}}
====Yunohara==== [[File:湯の原のお船（せり上げ前）.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yunohara ofune (roof lowered)]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Shiraito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige; Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane (Takusai) * '''Built''' : Started Ansei 2 (1855), completed Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Total cost''' : 736 ryō 3 bu + 108 mon * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|ja|二十四孝|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1851166}} (Tang Lady nursing her mother, Yang Xiang and a tiger, Tan Zi and the hunter, Emperor Shun and an elephant), “Millet Ears with Quail,” lions, dragons, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From the top—blue-green, red, yellow-green * '''Features''' : Roof is a raisable “seri.” Extremely elaborate carving; black lacquered axle bed; gold-leaf coffered ceiling on the second storey. The green metalwork, showing dragons and clouds, is by Kyoto artisan Taia-mi Sakuzaemon. The Yunohara youths who haul this float are famous for their spirit and form the festival’s core. {{-}}
====Arai==== [[File:新井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Arai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Wakakusa-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Chinese lion, {{ill|Seven Sages|ja|七賢人|1=Seven Sages of Greece|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207782}}, {{ill|Ma Shihuang|ja|馬師皇}}, dragon, carp in a torrent, tortoise in a torrent, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Relief-style carving distinct from the Tachikawa school; roof is raisable. {{-}}
====Shimokanai==== [[File:下金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shimokanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kaneko-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Harada Sōkei, disciple of Tomitane * '''Built''' : Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q32613}}, {{ill|Tokiwa Gozen|ja|常盤御前|1=Tokiwa Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q867354}}, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Ushiwakamaru|lt_ja=牛若丸}} and {{ill|Musashibō Benkei|ja|武蔵坊弁慶|lt=Benkei|lt_ja=弁慶|1=Musashibō Benkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q316898}} on Gojō Bridge, “Millet Ears with Quail,” bamboo and tiger, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Gold-leaf “cloud ceiling” beneath the eaves of the second storey; “Kemari-shishi” lions (ceiling); “Elegant Gentlemen at Leisure” (lower tier) are original. {{-}}
====Aramachi==== [[File:荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Akabito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Tōkei|ja|立川東渓}} (Shimizu Tōkei) and his son Shimizu Yūsui (1926) * '''Built''' : Probably early Meiji; one account claims Meiwa 4 (1767), but no reliable source confirms this. * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}}, Minamoto no Yoritomo, {{ill|Nio|ja|力神}}, {{ill|Fenghuang|ja|鳳凰|1=fenghuang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q527525}}, {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}}, {{ill|Fūjin|ja|風神}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Most carvings are by Tōkei. The purple dragon curtain on the second storey was sewn in Bunsei 12 (1829). In Meiji 2 (1869) a red foreign broadcloth curtain was imported from Britain and added. {{-}}
====Nishi-Aramachi==== [[File:西荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nishi-Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kayano-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Shimizu Yūsui * '''Built''' : Taishō 11 (1922) * '''Carvings''' : ''Kuni-umashi Monogatari'', ''Kamado no Kemuri'', dragons, birds-and-flowers, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. {{-}}
====Kamikanai==== [[File:上金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kamikanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Enjō-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Tachikawa Tōkei (mid-Meiji) * '''Built''' : Before Tenpō 6 (1835) * '''Carvings''' : Ascending dragon, descending dragon, carp in rapids, tortoise in waves, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : The only ofune that displays a figure ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}) on the second storey. The ascending/descending dragons on the roof pillars are especially striking. Probably the oldest float on record. {{-}}
====Fujii==== [[File:藤井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fujii ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Asahi-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Tomiyasu * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Hotei and karako children, Bishamonten and Benzaiten, {{ill|Lin Hejing|ja|林和靖|1=Lin Bu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q285429}}, immortals, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From top—red, black, red, white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Underwent major repairs in 2007–08. {{-}}
====Tokawaji==== [[File:兎川寺のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tokawaji ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyako-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unspecified Tachikawa-school artists * '''Supplementary carving''' : Shimizu Shimata-rō, second son of Tōkei (1928) * '''Built''' : Around the Tenpō era (1830s?) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木公}}, {{ill|Murakami Yoshimitsu|ja|村上義光|1=Murakami Yoshiteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522887}}, treasuries motif, {{ill|Lu Ao|ja|盧敖}}, mice and daikon radish, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Underwent major repairs in 2023–24. {{-}}
==Issues== * More than a century has passed since most floats were built, and carvings are cracked or broken; wheels tilt and overall damage is severe. Restoration is extremely costly and beyond the means of the individual neighbourhood associations. * In the 2000s outsiders began boarding floats and committing acts of violence, disrupting the festival. In 2014 the organising committee adopted a resolution excluding {{ill|Japanese organized crime group|ja|暴力団|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465|lt=organized crime groups|1=Japanese organized crime group}} and strictly forbade anyone but designated participants from boarding.<ref>“昨年に続き暴力排除決議 実行委と祭典委.” 『長野日報』25 June 2015.</ref>
==Notes== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==External links== * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1+index.id+78.htm Susukigawa Shrine Grand Festival “Ofune Matsuri”] – Official Matsumoto tourism site * [http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/prefecture/058.html Satoyamabe Ofune Matsuri Floats] – “Treasures of Matsumoto” (Matsumoto City Board of Education) * [http://www3.ncp.or.jp/cgi-shl/pbcgi60J.exe/BUNKA/u_tools/f_dsp_bz/?2938 Satoyamabe Ofune Festival Floats]{{リンク切れ|date=March 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }} – Nagano Cultural Properties (Hachijūni Cultural Foundation) * {{YouTube|ayNkT8kd5oM|Ofune Nine Boats Entering the Shrine}}
{{event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ofune Matsuri}}

'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.

Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.

An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.

====Shimokanai==== [[File:下金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shimokanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kaneko-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Harada Sōkei, disciple of Tomitane * '''Built''' : Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q32613}}, {{ill|Tokiwa Gozen|ja|常盤御前|1=Tokiwa Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q867354}}, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Ushiwakamaru|lt_ja=牛若丸}} and {{ill|Musashibō Benkei|ja|武蔵坊弁慶|lt=Benkei|lt_ja=弁慶|1=Musashibō Benkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q316898}} on Gojō Bridge, “Millet Ears with Quail,” bamboo and tiger, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Gold-leaf “cloud ceiling” beneath the eaves of the second storey; “Kemari-shishi” lions (ceiling); “Elegant Gentlemen at Leisure” (lower tier) are original. {{-}}
====Aramachi==== [[File:荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Akabito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Tōkei|ja|立川東渓}} (Shimizu Tōkei) and his son Shimizu Yūsui (1926) * '''Built''' : Probably early Meiji; one account claims Meiwa 4 (1767), but no reliable source confirms this. * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}}, Minamoto no Yoritomo, {{ill|Nio|ja|力神}}, {{ill|Fenghuang|ja|鳳凰|1=fenghuang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q527525}}, {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}}, {{ill|Fūjin|ja|風神}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Most carvings are by Tōkei. The purple dragon curtain on the second storey was sewn in Bunsei 12 (1829). In Meiji 2 (1869) a red foreign broadcloth curtain was imported from Britain and added. {{-}}
====Nishi-Aramachi==== [[File:西荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nishi-Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kayano-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Shimizu Yūsui * '''Built''' : Taishō 11 (1922) * '''Carvings''' : ''Kuni-umashi Monogatari'', ''Kamado no Kemuri'', dragons, birds-and-flowers, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. {{-}}
====Kamikanai==== [[File:上金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kamikanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Enjō-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Tachikawa Tōkei (mid-Meiji) * '''Built''' : Before Tenpō 6 (1835) * '''Carvings''' : Ascending dragon, descending dragon, carp in rapids, tortoise in waves, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : The only ofune that displays a figure ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}) on the second storey. The ascending/descending dragons on the roof pillars are especially striking. Probably the oldest float on record. {{-}}
====Fujii==== [[File:藤井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fujii ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Asahi-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Tomiyasu * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Hotei and karako children, Bishamonten and Benzaiten, {{ill|Lin Hejing|ja|林和靖|1=Lin Bu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q285429}}, immortals, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From top—red, black, red, white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Underwent major repairs in 2007–08. {{-}}
====Tokawaji==== [[File:兎川寺のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tokawaji ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyako-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unspecified Tachikawa-school artists * '''Supplementary carving''' : Shimizu Shimata-rō, second son of Tōkei (1928) * '''Built''' : Around the Tenpō era (1830s?) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木公}}, {{ill|Murakami Yoshimitsu|ja|村上義光|1=Murakami Yoshiteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522887}}, treasuries motif, {{ill|Lu Ao|ja|盧敖}}, mice and daikon radish, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Underwent major repairs in 2023–24. {{-}}
==Issues== * More than a century has passed since most floats were built, and carvings are cracked or broken; wheels tilt and overall damage is severe. Restoration is extremely costly and beyond the means of the individual neighbourhood associations. * In the 2000s outsiders began boarding floats and committing acts of violence, disrupting the festival. In 2014 the organising committee adopted a resolution excluding {{ill|Japanese organized crime group|ja|暴力団|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465|lt=organized crime groups|1=Japanese organized crime group}} and strictly forbade anyone but designated participants from boarding.<ref>“昨年に続き暴力排除決議 実行委と祭典委.” 『長野日報』25 June 2015.</ref>

<!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--> [[Category:Float festivals of Japan]] [[Category:Culture of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Festivals in Nagano Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11263357]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ref improve|date=October 10, 2015}} {{otheruses |festival at Susukigawa Shrine in Matsumoto, Nagano Prefecture |festival at {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|1=Hotaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597109}} in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|1=Azumino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534667}} |Hotaka Shrine#御船祭 }} {{Pathnav|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|frame=1}} [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An “ofune” passing energetically beneath the torii]]
'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.
==Overview== The festival traditionally announces the start of {{ill|Rice planting|ja|田植え|1=rice transplantation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12595244}} in the Yamabe area and prays for an abundant {{ill|Harvest|ja|豊作|1=Bumper crop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4997332}} in autumn. Because Susukigawa Shrine’s deities are not sea-gods, the custom began only in the Edo period, modelled on maritime rites elsewhere.
Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.
==Object of Worship== The Yamabe district lies along the {{ill|Usu River|ja|薄川|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}}, which has long been revered. The Ofune Matsuri is believed to carry the river’s blessings to every neighbourhood.
An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.
==About the “ofune”==
===Structure=== Each float is roughly 5 m high, 2.82 m wide, and 4.44 m deep (dimensions vary by association). For the festival a carrying-pole is lashed to either side and curtains are draped all around so that the float sways like a great ship, making it look even larger. There is only one axle with a pair of wheels. The second storey is open on all sides, while the first is richly carved; the axle bed is lavishly lacquered and fitted with ornate metalwork. Some floats have roofs (seri) that can be raised and lowered. This style of float is extremely rare nationwide.
===Specifications=== The floats are described below in the order in which they proceed.
====Usu-machi==== [[File:薄町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Usu-machi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyamoto'' or ''Kataha-sha'' * '''Carver''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige|ja|立川和四郎富重}}, {{ill|Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane|ja|立川専四郎富種}} and his daughter Matsushiro (Shōran) * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Li Tieguai|ja|李鉄拐|lt=Tekkai Sennin|lt_ja=鐡拐仙人|1=Iron-Crutch Li|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1473554}}, 唐獅子 (Chinese lion), {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, “Millet Ears with Quail,” {{ill|Gama Sennin|ja|蝦蟇仙人|1=Gama Sennin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625785}}, {{ill|Dongfang Shuo|ja|東方朔|1=Dongfang Shuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190966}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Like Yunohara, especially rich in carvings. A plan to display a figure of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} on the second storey was never completed. {{-}}
====Yunohara==== [[File:湯の原のお船（せり上げ前）.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yunohara ofune (roof lowered)]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Shiraito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige; Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane (Takusai) * '''Built''' : Started Ansei 2 (1855), completed Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Total cost''' : 736 ryō 3 bu + 108 mon * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|ja|二十四孝|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1851166}} (Tang Lady nursing her mother, Yang Xiang and a tiger, Tan Zi and the hunter, Emperor Shun and an elephant), “Millet Ears with Quail,” lions, dragons, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From the top—blue-green, red, yellow-green * '''Features''' : Roof is a raisable “seri.” Extremely elaborate carving; black lacquered axle bed; gold-leaf coffered ceiling on the second storey. The green metalwork, showing dragons and clouds, is by Kyoto artisan Taia-mi Sakuzaemon. The Yunohara youths who haul this float are famous for their spirit and form the festival’s core. {{-}}
====Arai==== [[File:新井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Arai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Wakakusa-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Chinese lion, {{ill|Seven Sages|ja|七賢人|1=Seven Sages of Greece|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207782}}, {{ill|Ma Shihuang|ja|馬師皇}}, dragon, carp in a torrent, tortoise in a torrent, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Relief-style carving distinct from the Tachikawa school; roof is raisable. {{-}}
====Shimokanai==== [[File:下金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shimokanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kaneko-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Harada Sōkei, disciple of Tomitane * '''Built''' : Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q32613}}, {{ill|Tokiwa Gozen|ja|常盤御前|1=Tokiwa Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q867354}}, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Ushiwakamaru|lt_ja=牛若丸}} and {{ill|Musashibō Benkei|ja|武蔵坊弁慶|lt=Benkei|lt_ja=弁慶|1=Musashibō Benkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q316898}} on Gojō Bridge, “Millet Ears with Quail,” bamboo and tiger, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Gold-leaf “cloud ceiling” beneath the eaves of the second storey; “Kemari-shishi” lions (ceiling); “Elegant Gentlemen at Leisure” (lower tier) are original. {{-}}
====Aramachi==== [[File:荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Akabito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Tōkei|ja|立川東渓}} (Shimizu Tōkei) and his son Shimizu Yūsui (1926) * '''Built''' : Probably early Meiji; one account claims Meiwa 4 (1767), but no reliable source confirms this. * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}}, Minamoto no Yoritomo, {{ill|Nio|ja|力神}}, {{ill|Fenghuang|ja|鳳凰|1=fenghuang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q527525}}, {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}}, {{ill|Fūjin|ja|風神}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Most carvings are by Tōkei. The purple dragon curtain on the second storey was sewn in Bunsei 12 (1829). In Meiji 2 (1869) a red foreign broadcloth curtain was imported from Britain and added. {{-}}
====Nishi-Aramachi==== [[File:西荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nishi-Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kayano-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Shimizu Yūsui * '''Built''' : Taishō 11 (1922) * '''Carvings''' : ''Kuni-umashi Monogatari'', ''Kamado no Kemuri'', dragons, birds-and-flowers, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. {{-}}
====Kamikanai==== [[File:上金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kamikanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Enjō-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Tachikawa Tōkei (mid-Meiji) * '''Built''' : Before Tenpō 6 (1835) * '''Carvings''' : Ascending dragon, descending dragon, carp in rapids, tortoise in waves, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : The only ofune that displays a figure ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}) on the second storey. The ascending/descending dragons on the roof pillars are especially striking. Probably the oldest float on record. {{-}}

Underwent major repairs in 2007–08. {{-}}

'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.

Because Susukigawa Shrine’s deities are not sea-gods, the custom began only in the Edo period, modelled on maritime rites elsewhere.
Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.
==Object of Worship== The Yamabe district lies along the {{ill|Usu River|ja|薄川|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}}, which has long been revered. The Ofune Matsuri is believed to carry the river’s blessings to every neighbourhood.
An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.
==About the “ofune”==
===Structure=== Each float is roughly 5 m high, 2.82 m wide, and 4.44 m deep (dimensions vary by association). For the festival a carrying-pole is lashed to either side and curtains are draped all around so that the float sways like a great ship, making it look even larger. There is only one axle with a pair of wheels. The second storey is open on all sides, while the first is richly carved; the axle bed is lavishly lacquered and fitted with ornate metalwork. Some floats have roofs (seri) that can be raised and lowered. This style of float is extremely rare nationwide.
===Specifications=== The floats are described below in the order in which they proceed.
====Usu-machi==== [[File:薄町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Usu-machi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyamoto'' or ''Kataha-sha'' * '''Carver''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige|ja|立川和四郎富重}}, {{ill|Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane|ja|立川専四郎富種}} and his daughter Matsushiro (Shōran) * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Li Tieguai|ja|李鉄拐|lt=Tekkai Sennin|lt_ja=鐡拐仙人|1=Iron-Crutch Li|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1473554}}, 唐獅子 (Chinese lion), {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, “Millet Ears with Quail,” {{ill|Gama Sennin|ja|蝦蟇仙人|1=Gama Sennin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625785}}, {{ill|Dongfang Shuo|ja|東方朔|1=Dongfang Shuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190966}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Like Yunohara, especially rich in carvings. A plan to display a figure of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} on the second storey was never completed. {{-}}
====Yunohara==== [[File:湯の原のお船（せり上げ前）.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yunohara ofune (roof lowered)]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Shiraito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige; Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane (Takusai) * '''Built''' : Started Ansei 2 (1855), completed Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Total cost''' : 736 ryō 3 bu + 108 mon * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|ja|二十四孝|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1851166}} (Tang Lady nursing her mother, Yang Xiang and a tiger, Tan Zi and the hunter, Emperor Shun and an elephant), “Millet Ears with Quail,” lions, dragons, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From the top—blue-green, red, yellow-green * '''Features''' : Roof is a raisable “seri.” Extremely elaborate carving; black lacquered axle bed; gold-leaf coffered ceiling on the second storey. The green metalwork, showing dragons and clouds, is by Kyoto artisan Taia-mi Sakuzaemon. The Yunohara youths who haul this float are famous for their spirit and form the festival’s core. {{-}}
====Arai==== [[File:新井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Arai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Wakakusa-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Chinese lion, {{ill|Seven Sages|ja|七賢人|1=Seven Sages of Greece|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207782}}, {{ill|Ma Shihuang|ja|馬師皇}}, dragon, carp in a torrent, tortoise in a torrent, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Relief-style carving distinct from the Tachikawa school; roof is raisable. {{-}}
====Shimokanai==== [[File:下金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shimokanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kaneko-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Harada Sōkei, disciple of Tomitane * '''Built''' : Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q32613}}, {{ill|Tokiwa Gozen|ja|常盤御前|1=Tokiwa Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q867354}}, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Ushiwakamaru|lt_ja=牛若丸}} and {{ill|Musashibō Benkei|ja|武蔵坊弁慶|lt=Benkei|lt_ja=弁慶|1=Musashibō Benkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q316898}} on Gojō Bridge, “Millet Ears with Quail,” bamboo and tiger, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Gold-leaf “cloud ceiling” beneath the eaves of the second storey; “Kemari-shishi” lions (ceiling); “Elegant Gentlemen at Leisure” (lower tier) are original. {{-}}
====Aramachi==== [[File:荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Akabito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Tōkei|ja|立川東渓}} (Shimizu Tōkei) and his son Shimizu Yūsui (1926) * '''Built''' : Probably early Meiji; one account claims Meiwa 4 (1767), but no reliable source confirms this. * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}}, Minamoto no Yoritomo, {{ill|Nio|ja|力神}}, {{ill|Fenghuang|ja|鳳凰|1=fenghuang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q527525}}, {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}}, {{ill|Fūjin|ja|風神}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Most carvings are by Tōkei. The purple dragon curtain on the second storey was sewn in Bunsei 12 (1829). In Meiji 2 (1869) a red foreign broadcloth curtain was imported from Britain and added. {{-}}
====Nishi-Aramachi==== [[File:西荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nishi-Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kayano-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Shimizu Yūsui * '''Built''' : Taishō 11 (1922) * '''Carvings''' : ''Kuni-umashi Monogatari'', ''Kamado no Kemuri'', dragons, birds-and-flowers, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. {{-}}
====Kamikanai==== [[File:上金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kamikanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Enjō-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Tachikawa Tōkei (mid-Meiji) * '''Built''' : Before Tenpō 6 (1835) * '''Carvings''' : Ascending dragon, descending dragon, carp in rapids, tortoise in waves, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : The only ofune that displays a figure ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}) on the second storey. The ascending/descending dragons on the roof pillars are especially striking. Probably the oldest float on record. {{-}}
====Fujii==== [[File:藤井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fujii ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Asahi-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Tomiyasu * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Hotei and karako children, Bishamonten and Benzaiten, {{ill|Lin Hejing|ja|林和靖|1=Lin Bu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q285429}}, immortals, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From top—red, black, red, white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Underwent major repairs in 2007–08. {{-}}
====Tokawaji==== [[File:兎川寺のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tokawaji ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyako-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unspecified Tachikawa-school artists * '''Supplementary carving''' : Shimizu Shimata-rō, second son of Tōkei (1928) * '''Built''' : Around the Tenpō era (1830s?) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木公}}, {{ill|Murakami Yoshimitsu|ja|村上義光|1=Murakami Yoshiteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522887}}, treasuries motif, {{ill|Lu Ao|ja|盧敖}}, mice and daikon radish, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Underwent major repairs in 2023–24. {{-}}
==Issues== * More than a century has passed since most floats were built, and carvings are cracked or broken; wheels tilt and overall damage is severe. Restoration is extremely costly and beyond the means of the individual neighbourhood associations. * In the 2000s outsiders began boarding floats and committing acts of violence, disrupting the festival. In 2014 the organising committee adopted a resolution excluding {{ill|Japanese organized crime group|ja|暴力団|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465|lt=organized crime groups|1=Japanese organized crime group}} and strictly forbade anyone but designated participants from boarding.<ref>“昨年に続き暴力排除決議 実行委と祭典委.” 『長野日報』25 June 2015.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11263357]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ref improve|date=October 10, 2015}} {{otheruses |festival at Susukigawa Shrine in Matsumoto, Nagano Prefecture |festival at {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|1=Hotaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597109}} in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|1=Azumino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534667}} |Hotaka Shrine#御船祭 }} {{Pathnav|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|frame=1}} [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An “ofune” passing energetically beneath the torii]]
'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.
==Overview== The festival traditionally announces the start of {{ill|Rice planting|ja|田植え|1=rice transplantation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12595244}} in the Yamabe area and prays for an abundant {{ill|Harvest|ja|豊作|1=Bumper crop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4997332}} in autumn. Because Susukigawa Shrine’s deities are not sea-gods, the custom began only in the Edo period, modelled on maritime rites elsewhere.
Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.
==Object of Worship== The Yamabe district lies along the {{ill|Usu River|ja|薄川|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}}, which has long been revered. The Ofune Matsuri is believed to carry the river’s blessings to every neighbourhood.
An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.
==About the “ofune”==
===Structure=== Each float is roughly 5 m high, 2.82 m wide, and 4.44 m deep (dimensions vary by association). For the festival a carrying-pole is lashed to either side and curtains are draped all around so that the float sways like a great ship, making it look even larger. There is only one axle with a pair of wheels. The second storey is open on all sides, while the first is richly carved; the axle bed is lavishly lacquered and fitted with ornate metalwork. Some floats have roofs (seri) that can be raised and lowered. This style of float is extremely rare nationwide.
===Specifications=== The floats are described below in the order in which they proceed.

[[ja:岩村田祇園祭]]
<!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--><!--Ship Festival (Susukigisui Shrine)--> [[Category:Float festivals of Japan]] [[Category:Culture of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Festivals in Nagano Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11263357}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お船祭り (須々岐水神社)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11263357]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ref improve|date=October 10, 2015}} {{otheruses |festival at Susukigawa Shrine in Matsumoto, Nagano Prefecture |festival at {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|1=Hotaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597109}} in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|1=Azumino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q534667}} |Hotaka Shrine#御船祭 }} {{Pathnav|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|frame=1}} [[File:鳥居を勢い良く潜るお船.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An “ofune” passing energetically beneath the torii]]
'''Ofune Matsuri''' (おふねまつり, “Boat Festival”) is a {{ill|Festival|ja|祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} held every year on {{ill|5 May|ja|5月5日|1=May 5|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2550}} (on 4 May in {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|1=Onbashira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3698846}} years—i.e. zodiac Years of the Hare and Rooster) at {{ill|Susukigawa Shrine (Matsumoto)|ja|須々岐水神社 (松本市)|1=Susukigisui Shrine (Matsumoto City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664481}} in the Satoyamabe district of {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|1=Matsumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q213324}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|1=Nagano Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127877}}.
==Overview== The festival traditionally announces the start of {{ill|Rice planting|ja|田植え|1=rice transplantation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12595244}} in the Yamabe area and prays for an abundant {{ill|Harvest|ja|豊作|1=Bumper crop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4997332}} in autumn. Because Susukigawa Shrine’s deities are not sea-gods, the custom began only in the Edo period, modelled on maritime rites elsewhere.
Nine neighbourhood associations—Usu-machi, Yunohara, Arai, Shimokanai, Aramachi, Nishi-Aramachi, Kamikanai, Fujii, and Tokawaji—each own one festival float (“ofune”). These {{ill|Festival float|ja|山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} were collectively designated a Prefectural Treasure on 25 August 1986 and draw attention for their great mass and the vigour of the young men who manoeuvre them. Some associations parade their floats through their own districts on the day before the festival.
==Object of Worship== The Yamabe district lies along the {{ill|Usu River|ja|薄川|1=Susuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622154}}, which has long been revered. The Ofune Matsuri is believed to carry the river’s blessings to every neighbourhood.
An entry for the 12th lunar-month, “{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌}}” day of Enryaku 18 (AD 799) in the {{ill|Nihon Kōki|ja|日本後紀|1=Nihon Kōki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1077029}} records that the immigrant Keru Manro of Shinano Province, descended from {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|1=Goguryeo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28370}}, was granted the surname “Susuki.” That name survives in Susukigawa Shrine (“Susuki-gawa” = Usu River), and there is still a district called Usu-machi derived from the same river.
==About the “ofune”==
===Structure=== Each float is roughly 5 m high, 2.82 m wide, and 4.44 m deep (dimensions vary by association). For the festival a carrying-pole is lashed to either side and curtains are draped all around so that the float sways like a great ship, making it look even larger. There is only one axle with a pair of wheels. The second storey is open on all sides, while the first is richly carved; the axle bed is lavishly lacquered and fitted with ornate metalwork. Some floats have roofs (seri) that can be raised and lowered. This style of float is extremely rare nationwide.
===Specifications=== The floats are described below in the order in which they proceed.
====Usu-machi==== [[File:薄町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Usu-machi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyamoto'' or ''Kataha-sha'' * '''Carver''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige|ja|立川和四郎富重}}, {{ill|Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane|ja|立川専四郎富種}} and his daughter Matsushiro (Shōran) * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Li Tieguai|ja|李鉄拐|lt=Tekkai Sennin|lt_ja=鐡拐仙人|1=Iron-Crutch Li|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1473554}}, 唐獅子 (Chinese lion), {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, “Millet Ears with Quail,” {{ill|Gama Sennin|ja|蝦蟇仙人|1=Gama Sennin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625785}}, {{ill|Dongfang Shuo|ja|東方朔|1=Dongfang Shuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190966}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Like Yunohara, especially rich in carvings. A plan to display a figure of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} on the second storey was never completed. {{-}}
====Yunohara==== [[File:湯の原のお船（せり上げ前）.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yunohara ofune (roof lowered)]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Shiraito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Washirō Tomishige; Tachikawa Senjirō Tomitane (Takusai) * '''Built''' : Started Ansei 2 (1855), completed Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Total cost''' : 736 ryō 3 bu + 108 mon * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|ja|二十四孝|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1851166}} (Tang Lady nursing her mother, Yang Xiang and a tiger, Tan Zi and the hunter, Emperor Shun and an elephant), “Millet Ears with Quail,” lions, dragons, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From the top—blue-green, red, yellow-green * '''Features''' : Roof is a raisable “seri.” Extremely elaborate carving; black lacquered axle bed; gold-leaf coffered ceiling on the second storey. The green metalwork, showing dragons and clouds, is by Kyoto artisan Taia-mi Sakuzaemon. The Yunohara youths who haul this float are famous for their spirit and form the festival’s core. {{-}}
====Arai==== [[File:新井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Arai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Wakakusa-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Chinese lion, {{ill|Seven Sages|ja|七賢人|1=Seven Sages of Greece|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207782}}, {{ill|Ma Shihuang|ja|馬師皇}}, dragon, carp in a torrent, tortoise in a torrent, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Relief-style carving distinct from the Tachikawa school; roof is raisable. {{-}}
====Shimokanai==== [[File:下金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shimokanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kaneko-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Harada Sōkei, disciple of Tomitane * '''Built''' : Ansei 4 (1857) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q32613}}, {{ill|Tokiwa Gozen|ja|常盤御前|1=Tokiwa Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q867354}}, {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|ja|新田義貞}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|lt=Ushiwakamaru|lt_ja=牛若丸}} and {{ill|Musashibō Benkei|ja|武蔵坊弁慶|lt=Benkei|lt_ja=弁慶|1=Musashibō Benkei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q316898}} on Gojō Bridge, “Millet Ears with Quail,” bamboo and tiger, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : Gold-leaf “cloud ceiling” beneath the eaves of the second storey; “Kemari-shishi” lions (ceiling); “Elegant Gentlemen at Leisure” (lower tier) are original. {{-}}
====Aramachi==== [[File:荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Akabito-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : {{ill|Tachikawa Tōkei|ja|立川東渓}} (Shimizu Tōkei) and his son Shimizu Yūsui (1926) * '''Built''' : Probably early Meiji; one account claims Meiwa 4 (1767), but no reliable source confirms this. * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|ja|石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}}, Minamoto no Yoritomo, {{ill|Nio|ja|力神}}, {{ill|Fenghuang|ja|鳳凰|1=fenghuang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q527525}}, {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}}, {{ill|Fūjin|ja|風神}}, &c. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Most carvings are by Tōkei. The purple dragon curtain on the second storey was sewn in Bunsei 12 (1829). In Meiji 2 (1869) a red foreign broadcloth curtain was imported from Britain and added. {{-}}
====Nishi-Aramachi==== [[File:西荒町のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nishi-Aramachi ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Kayano-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Shimizu Yūsui * '''Built''' : Taishō 11 (1922) * '''Carvings''' : ''Kuni-umashi Monogatari'', ''Kamado no Kemuri'', dragons, birds-and-flowers, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. {{-}}
====Kamikanai==== [[File:上金井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kamikanai ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Enjō-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unknown * '''Supplementary carving''' : Tachikawa Tōkei (mid-Meiji) * '''Built''' : Before Tenpō 6 (1835) * '''Carvings''' : Ascending dragon, descending dragon, carp in rapids, tortoise in waves, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Features''' : The only ofune that displays a figure ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}) on the second storey. The ascending/descending dragons on the roof pillars are especially striking. Probably the oldest float on record. {{-}}
====Fujii==== [[File:藤井のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fujii ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Asahi-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Tachikawa Tomiyasu * '''Built''' : Late Edo period * '''Carvings''' : Hotei and karako children, Bishamonten and Benzaiten, {{ill|Lin Hejing|ja|林和靖|1=Lin Bu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q285429}}, immortals, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : From top—red, black, red, white * '''Other''' : Roof raisable. Underwent major repairs in 2007–08. {{-}}
====Tokawaji==== [[File:兎川寺のお船.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tokawaji ofune]] * '''Guild name''' : ''Miyako-ren'' * '''Carver''' : Unspecified Tachikawa-school artists * '''Supplementary carving''' : Shimizu Shimata-rō, second son of Tōkei (1928) * '''Built''' : Around the Tenpō era (1830s?) * '''Carvings''' : {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木公}}, {{ill|Murakami Yoshimitsu|ja|村上義光|1=Murakami Yoshiteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522887}}, treasuries motif, {{ill|Lu Ao|ja|盧敖}}, mice and daikon radish, etc. * '''Curtain colours''' : Red and white * '''Other''' : Underwent major repairs in 2023–24. {{-}}
==Issues== * More than a century has passed since most floats were built, and carvings are cracked or broken; wheels tilt and overall damage is severe. Restoration is extremely costly and beyond the means of the individual neighbourhood associations. * In the 2000s outsiders began boarding floats and committing acts of violence, disrupting the festival. In 2014 the organising committee adopted a resolution excluding {{ill|Japanese organized crime group|ja|暴力団|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11090465|lt=organized crime groups|1=Japanese organized crime group}} and strictly forbade anyone but designated participants from boarding.<ref>“昨年に続き暴力排除決議 実行委と祭典委.” 『長野日報』25 June 2015.</ref>
==Notes== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==External links== * [http://youkoso.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/special1+index.id+78.htm Susukigawa Shrine Grand Festival “Ofune Matsuri”] – Official Matsumoto tourism site * [http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/prefecture/058.html Satoyamabe Ofune Matsuri Floats] – “Treasures of Matsumoto” (Matsumoto City Board of Education) * [http://www3.ncp.or.jp/cgi-shl/pbcgi60J.exe/BUNKA/u_tools/f_dsp_bz/?2938 Satoyamabe Ofune Festival Floats]{{リンク切れ|date=March 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }} – Nagano Cultural Properties (Hachijūni Cultural Foundation) * {{YouTube|ayNkT8kd5oM|Ofune Nine Boats Entering the Shrine}}
